name: Crypto.Strings

values.name: decrypt
values.comment:
Decrypt a string created with `encrypt`.

See `Crypto.Strings.Crypt.decrypt` for more options.

This can get errors if the ciphertext you pass in decrypts to something that isn't a UTF-8 string.

values.name: encrypt
values.comment:
Encrypt a string. Encode the output as Base64 with 80-character lines.

The `Seed` parameter is a `Random.Seed`, as created by `Random.initialSeed`

See `Crypto.Strings.Crypt.encrypt` for more options.

This shouldn't ever return an error, but since the key generation can possibly do so, it returns a `Result` instead of just `(Ciphertext, randomState)`.
values.type: Seed -> Passphrase -> Plaintext -> Result String ( Ciphertext, Seed )

values.name: justEncrypt
values.comment: Testing function. Just returns the result with no random generator update.

name: Crypto.Strings.Crypt

aliases.name: DefaultKey
aliases.comment: Default key type.

values.name: blocksToList
values.comment: Convert a list of blocks into a list of integers.

values.name: decrypt
values.comment: Decrypt a string created with `encrypt`.
values.type: Config key state randomState -> Key key -> Ciphertext -> Result Plaintext String

values.name: defaultConfig
values.comment: Default configuration.

values.name: encrypt
values.comment: Encrypt a string.
values.type: Config key state randomState -> RandomGenerator randomState -> Key key -> Plaintext -> ( Ciphertext, randomState )

values.name: expandKeyString
values.comment: Expand a key preparing it for use with `encrypt` or `decrypt`.
values.type: Config key state randomState -> Passphrase -> Result String (Key key)

values.name: listToBlocks
values.comment:
Convert a list of integers into a list of blocks.

The end of the last block will always be #x80 plus zeroes. If all the
blocks are full, and the last byte is NOT zero or #x80, then no
padding is added.

values.name: padLastBlock
values.comment:
Put a 0x80 at the end of the last block, and pad with zeroes.

No padding is done if the last block is of the blockSize, and does NOT already end with 0 or 0x80.

values.name: seedGenerator
values.comment: A random generator that takes and returns a standard Elm Seed.

values.name: stripTrailingZeroes
values.comment: Strip the trailing zeroes from a block.

values.name: unpadLastBlock
values.comment: Remove the padding added by `padLastBlock` from the last block in a list.

name: Crypto.Strings.Encoding

values.name: base64Decoder
values.comment: Convert a Base64 string to bytes. Sometimes the string is malformed.

values.name: base64Encoder
values.comment: Convert bytes to Base64.

values.name: base64Encoding
values.comment:
How to encode/decode strings to/from Base64

The Int parameter is the line length for encoding to a string.

values.name: fold
values.comment:
Fold a list of integers to a specified size.

Actually XORs too-long pieces together to not lose any entropy.

Worth the effort? I don't know. Hashing probably does enough.

values.name: foldedSha256KeyEncoder
values.comment: Hash and fold a passphrase to turn it into a raw key array.

values.name: foldedSha256KeyEncoding
values.comment: A KeyEncoding for foldedSha256KeyEncoder

values.name: hexDecoder
values.comment: Convert a hex string to bytes. Sometimes the string is malformed.

values.name: hexEncoder
values.comment: Convert bytes to hex.

values.name: hexEncoding
values.comment: How to encode/decode strings to/from hex.

values.name: plainTextDecoder
values.comment: Decode UTF-8 bytes into a string.

values.name: plainTextEncoder
values.comment: Encode a string as UTF-8 bytes

name: Crypto.Strings.Chaining

aliases.name: CtrState
aliases.comment: The state for Counter chaining

aliases.name: EcbState
aliases.comment: The state for ECB chaining

values.name: ctrChaining
values.comment:
Counter Chaining

Uses the encryptor for both encryption and decryption.

values.name: ecbChaining
values.comment: Electronic Codebook chaining

name: Crypto.Strings.BlockAes

unions.name: KeySize
unions.comment: An AES key size. 16, 24, or 32 bytes.

aliases.name: Key
aliases.comment: AES key type

values.name: encryption
values.comment: AES encryption. 32-byte key size. Use `setKeySize` to change it.

values.name: setKeySize
values.comment: Change the key size of the keyExpander inside an AES Encryption spec.

name: Crypto.Strings.Types

unions.name: Key
unions.comment: A portable name for block encryption algorithm specific keys.

aliases.name: Block
aliases.comment: One block for a block encryption algorithm.

aliases.name: BlockSize
aliases.comment: An alternative name for Int to make docs clearer.

aliases.name: Chainer
aliases.comment: A block chaining algorithm.
aliases.type: state -> ( Encryptor key, Decryptor key ) -> key -> Block -> ( Block, state )

aliases.name: Chaining
aliases.comment: Package up all the information needed to do block chaining.
aliases.type: { name : String, initializer : ChainingInitializer state randomState, encryptor : Chainer key state, decryptor : Chainer key state, adjoiner : ChainingStateAdjoiner state, separator : ChainingStateSeparator state }

aliases.name: ChainingInitializer
aliases.comment: Create an initial chaining state for encryption.

aliases.name: ChainingStateAdjoiner
aliases.comment: Adjoin chaining state to a list of ciphertext blocks.

aliases.name: ChainingStateSeparator
aliases.comment: Remove the adjoined state from a list of cipher blocks and turn it into a state.
aliases.type: BlockSize -> List Int -> ( List Int, state )

aliases.name: Ciphertext
aliases.comment: Another name for `String`, to aid in documentation.

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment: Configuration for the block chaining and string encoding
aliases.type: { encryption : Encryption key, keyEncoding : KeyEncoding, chaining : Chaining key state randomState, encoding : Encoding }

aliases.name: Decoder
aliases.comment: A string decoding algorithm.

aliases.name: Decryptor
aliases.comment: A decryption function for a particular low-level block algorithm.

aliases.name: Encoder
aliases.comment: A string encoding algorithm.

aliases.name: Encoding
aliases.comment: Encoder and decoder for translating between strings and blocks.
aliases.type: { name : String, encoder : Encoder, decoder : Decoder }

aliases.name: Encryption
aliases.comment: Package up information about a block encryption algorithm.
aliases.type: { name : String, blockSize : BlockSize, keyExpander : KeyExpander key, encryptor : Encryptor key, decryptor : Decryptor key }

aliases.name: Encryptor
aliases.comment: An encryption function for a particular low-level block algorithm.

aliases.name: KeyEncoder
aliases.comment: Turn a passphrase into an IV.

aliases.name: KeyEncoding
aliases.comment:
Describe a `KeyEncoder` for a `Config`.

Not all `KeyEncodings` will be compatible with all block `Encryption` types.

aliases.name: KeyExpander
aliases.comment:
Describe key expansion for a particular block encryption algorithm.

`keySize` is the number of bytes in a raw key.

`expander` is a function to turn an array of that size into a key.
aliases.type: { keySize : Int, expander : Array Int -> Result String key }

aliases.name: Passphrase
aliases.comment: Another name for `String`, to aid in documentation.

aliases.name: Plaintext
aliases.comment: Another name for `String`, to aid in documentation.

aliases.name: RandomGenerator
aliases.comment: Create a random byte array of a given length
